scores.notes: Query asks which RPO handles Texas GI Bill claims. Answer directly provides the RPO city and mailing address. Perfectly concise and responsive.
Query asks the dollar threshold for four-signature review. Answer provides exactly that ($45,000). Direct and complete.
Query asks the MHA rate for distance-only learners under Ch 33. Answer gives the rate (50% national avg BAH) and effective date. Complete.
Query asks how MHA is calculated for a specific scenario. Answer gives the rate of pursuit calculation and the resulting BAH percentage directly.

Query asks for the benefit level for a veteran with exactly 800 days. Answer gives 80% and mentions the 70/80 rule caveat. Slightly complex due to the conditional note, but responsive. Minor: the '730-909 days qualifies for 80%' is the core answer and it's present.
Query asks what happened to the 40% benefit level. Answer directly states it was eliminated (August 1, 2020) and became the 50% level. Complete.
Query asks whether a veteran discharged Feb 1, 2014 has a delimiting date. Answer directly says no and explains the legal basis. Complete.
Query asks if Title 32 AGR service qualifies. Answer says yes, specifies effective date, qualifying types of service, and retroactive payment window. Fully responsive.
Query asks if bartending courses are covered. Answer gives a direct no with full scope. Complete.
Query asks about private pilot certificate at a flight school. Answer gives clear no for vocational and yes if credit toward a degree. Both parts addressed.
Query asks what medical certificate is required. Answer names the certificate class (second-class), timing (first day), and validity period (12 months). Complete.
Query asks if Chapter 35 beneficiaries can receive VA benefits for vocational flight training. Answer is direct: no, with Chapter 35 and Section 901 both called out.
Query asks minimum credits for 3/4-time status when 12 is full time. Answer is '9 credits.' Perfectly direct.
Query asks the training time classification for a specific credit/full-time combination. Answer gives 3/4 time with the applicable range. Direct and complete.
Query asks for the BDN training time code for Chapter 1606 full-time enrollment. Answer is 'Code 4.' Exactly responsive.
Query asks the date after which interval payments are no longer payable. Answer gives the date and cites the law. Complete.
Query asks when a summer-term interval exceeding 30 days is still payable. Answer gives the condition (at least 8 weeks). Direct.
Query asks what type of folder is used for Chapter 35 DEA claims. Answer gives the folder description (blue, two-part). Direct.
Query asks the suspense date for EPC 270. Answer gives 60 days and contextualizes versus other EPCs. Complete.
Query asks how many years of service are excluded for a ROTC-commissioned officer under Ch 33. Answer is '4 years.' Direct.
Query asks the excluded period for a Service Academy graduate. Answer gives '5 years from commissioning.' Direct and complete.
Query asks the maximum percentage covered by accelerated payment under Ch 30. Answer gives 60% of established tuition and fees for one term. Complete.
Query asks what happens to OJT housing allowance after year one under Ch 33. Answer walks through each 6-month step (100%→80%→60%→40%→20%). Directly and fully responsive.
Query asks the End Product Code for original Chapter 35 claims. Answer is 'EPC 240.' Direct.
Query asks the EPC for supplemental Chapter 1606 actions. Answer is 'EPC 280.' Direct.
Query asks which QA scorecard questions cover payment accuracy. Answer gives 'Questions 8 through 10.' Direct.
Query asks the date VA Education shifted from SAO to SOAR. Answer gives 'October 1, 2023.' Direct.
Query asks the five VADIR web service operations. Answer lists all five operations. Complete and direct.
Query asks the maximum response time requirement. Answer gives '2 seconds.' Direct.
Query asks about the confidentiality classification. Answer gives all three FIPS 199 ratings. Complete.
Query asks what field is required for findPeopleByCriteria. Answer names SSN and adds data format detail. Complete.
Query asks the default max results returned. Answer gives 50 (default) and 200 (hard cutoff). Complete.
Query asks what Character of Service code 'J' means. Answer gives the description and administrative context. Direct.
Query asks what VADIR project code '9GY' represents. Answer gives the full description (Hurricane Katrina, date, training indicator). Direct.
Query asks what VADIR service code represents Space Force. Answer is 'Code S.' Direct.
Query asks what getServicePeriods returns. Answer provides a comprehensive list of all data categories returned. Complete.
Query asks for the production endpoint URL. Answer gives the exact URL. Direct.
Query asks what MGSR status code 'CA' means. Answer gives the full description. Direct.
Query asks what the exclusionPerdTyp field contains. Answer gives both possible values and explains the meaning. Complete.
Query asks what error code means 'Invalid Search Criteria.' Answer gives the error code number and the callStatusNote text. Complete.

Multi-section query about Ch 33 eligibility with both ROTC and later active duty. Answer walks through the full determination process: ROTC exclusion, VADIR data lookup, benefit level chart, and delimiting date. Appropriately detailed for a multi-section query type.
Query asks the general delimiting date for Chapter 32. Answer is '10 years from discharge.' Direct.
Query asks if Chapter 35 surviving spouses have a delimiting date. Answer addresses surviving spouses (no, per CAVC) and also notes the contrast with regular Chapter 35 spouses. Fully responsive.
Query asks what VCE must check re: MGIB when Ch 33 entitlement exhausts. Answer directly states eligibility for the $1,200 MGIB refund (96R). Direct.
Query asks which RPO has jurisdiction for a guest/transient course. Answer directly states the degree-conferring school's RPO. Complete.
Query asks the transaction code for work study allowance. Answer is '06L.' Direct.
Query asks when Ch 33 payment review transitioned from BDN to eMPWR. Answer gives 'July 8, 2024.' Direct.
Query asks how long before inactive DGI user's record lock releases. Answer is '15 minutes.' Direct.

Query asks which law established two-certification and when it took effect for IHL beneficiaries. Answer names the law (PL 116-315 Section 1010), gives the NCD date (Phase 1: August 1, 2021), and the IHL date (Phase 2: December 17, 2021). Complete.

Complex multi-section query about the full VADIR system flow for Ch 33 eligibility. Answer describes all five operations in sequence and explains how the data drives each eligibility calculation step. Appropriately comprehensive and directly responsive.

Cross-source query asking about Character of Service 'H' and Ch 33 eligibility. Answer explains the code meaning, lists all qualifying codes, and concludes clearly that H qualifies. All parts addressed.
Cross-source query about project code '9GF' and qualifying service. Answer decodes the project code, explains the training indicator, and states whether it counts toward qualifying days. Complete.

Cross-source query about VADIR trainingPeriod data and M22-4 deduction rules. Answer explains what VADIR stores, the M22-4 730-day rule, and how VCEs cross-reference the two. Directly and fully responsive.

Query asks what eligibility decision the MGIB code and separation code inform. Answer explains what the fields represent and connects them to Ch 30 and the 96R refund. Mostly direct but mentions 'how they separated' broadly without fully pinpointing what specific eligibility decision the combination of both fields together informs, which was the exact question.
Query asks if a Purple Heart but short service affects the benefit percentage. Answer gives 100% directly, cites M22-4 rule, and explains how VADIR stores the Purple Heart indicator. Complete.

Cross-source query about which VADIR data element corresponds to the delimiting date concept. Answer names the specific field (eduPgmEntlDelimitDt in bdnEnt), explains M22-4 policy rules, and how the VCE reconciles them. Complete.
Query asks how VADIR ch1606Ent data relates to Ch 1606 eligibility. Answer explains the data elements, maps MGSR codes to eligibility states, and connects to M22-4 policy. Fully responsive.

Query asks what kickerRateCode 'B1' means and how VCE applies it. Answer gives the code description (marked OBSOLETE), explains how kickers work, and says to verify begin/end dates. Minor gap: the answer notes B1 is obsolete but doesn't fully explain how a VCE would handle an obsolete kicker code operationally — slightly incomplete on the 'how the VCE applies it' part.

Cross-source query about whether a veteran not on active Federal service is eligible for MHA. Answer explains activeServiceIndicator, applies the M22-4 MHA rule, and gives a clear conclusion. Complete.
Query asks about svcCd 'F' and statute 'A.' Answer correctly decodes both codes and draws the combined conclusion. Direct.

Three-source query about QA scorecard evaluation of VADIR data use. Answer identifies the relevant scorecard question (Q6), lists what it reviews, and explains how VADIR data interpretation errors would be caught. Complete.

Cross-source query about EPC for original Ch 33 processing and which VADIR operation provides data. Answer addresses both parts: explains there's no specific Ch 33 EPC in the table and names getServicePeriods as the VADIR operation. Complete.

Query asks to interpret specific field values (eduPgmEntlMtsQy=24, eduPgmTypCd='2'). Answer correctly explains 24 months remaining (12 used), but is vague on eduPgmTypCd '2' — says it 'maps to the program type code' without specifying what type '2' actually represents. Partial answer on one of the two fields.

Cross-source query about military academy 5-year obligation in Ch 33 calculation. Answer explains VADIR's militaryAcademyPeriod data, the M22-4 5-year exclusion, gives a concrete example, and describes the VCE's calculation. Fully responsive.

Query asks which VADIR data elements to check for 100% benefit via SC disability discharge. Answer names pgibCd/pgibSeparationCode and MGIB status code '01', plus the 30-day requirement. However, referencing 'mgad_loss_cat_codes (Table 48)' is slightly different from the standard Table 52 PGIB code approach, introducing minor confusion without a clear mapping. Core answer is present but slightly muddled.
Query asks how cipInd relates to Ch 33 benefit calculation. Answer explains the cip data element fields, the CIP program, and how VCEs subtract CIP periods from qualifying service. Complete.

Cross-source query about Ch 30 MGIB participant wanting to switch to Ch 33. Answer explains what ch30EnrlStatCd '03' means, explains the irrevocable election, and mentions the 96R refund. All implications addressed.

Query asks the fallback workflow when DGI finds no VADIR results. Answer describes the full three-step workflow with accurate details about findPeopleByCriteria and the 'Person Not Found' callStatusNote. Complete.

Three-source query about a veteran at a German university. Answer identifies the Buffalo RPO, explains the VADIR eligibility verification steps, and notes the foreign school MHA rate. All parts addressed.

Query asks what could cause different results from two VADIR pulls and what the system should do. Answer explains resultsHash, identifies possible causes, and describes the re-evaluation action. Complete.

Three-source query about QA verification of training time codes for nonstandard terms. Answer cites QA questions 8-10, references the specific M22-4 sections, and explains the equivalency table lookup. Fully responsive.

Query asks about VADIR education level code for baccalaureate and its effect on Ch 1606 MGSR status. Answer gives eduLvlCd '51', maps to MGSR 'CA', explains the suspended state, and notes VADIR corroborates the reason. Complete.

Query asks the big-pay threshold for Ch 1606 and which VADIR fields confirm participation. Answer gives both signature thresholds ($8K-$12K for three, $12K+ for four) and names the VADIR confirmation fields. Complete.

Query asks how VADIR PGIB separation code relates to the M22-4 30-day exception. Answer explains the code's meaning, names the two qualifying separation reasons, and directly states how the VCE uses it. Complete.

Three-source complex query about Title 32 AGR processing in eMPWR. Answer covers the qualifying service types, the relevant VADIR statute codes, project code distinction for AGR, and the eMPWR/BDN transition context. Comprehensive and responsive.

Consulting/SME query about where human judgment is still needed despite automated VADIR data. Answer identifies four specific categories with clear explanations for each. Appropriately detailed and directly answers the question.

Consulting/SME query about what happens if service record updates occur post-adjudication and how anyone would know. Answer explains resultsHash detection, the lack of automated triggers, M22-4 review requirements, M22-3 QA limitations, and the most likely detection path. Fully responsive and appropriately comprehensive.
Consulting/SME query asking for single points of failure. Answer identifies three clear SPOFs with specific supporting citations. Directly and fully responsive.
Consulting/SME query about data quality risks where valid service codes could produce wrong eligibility. Answer gives three specific risk areas with code-level examples. Directly responsive.

Consulting/SME query about whether QA distinguishes bad upstream data from VCE mistakes. Answer explains the 13-question structure, identifies Q6 as relevant, explains why the distinction isn't explicit in QA, and notes the sampling limitation. Fully responsive.

Consulting/SME query about downstream impact of slow VADIR service record lookups. Answer covers three downstream impact areas (LTS stalls, DGI locks, eMPWR), explains the blast radius, and distinguishes cached vs. new claims. Complete.

Consulting/SME query about data reconciliation challenges for reservist transitioning between Ch 1606 and Ch 33. Answer identifies three specific reconciliation challenges with VADIR field references and M22-4 context. Directly responsive.

Consulting/SME query about implications of VADIR ICD's 'codes will be added' note. Answer explains what happens when new codes arrive (descriptions lag), identifies three risk categories, and describes the structural maintenance debt. Fully responsive.

Consulting/SME query about detecting years-long wrong benefit levels from incomplete initial service record pull. Answer works through all four detection layer gaps and identifies the most realistic detection path. Complete and directly responsive.

Consulting/SME query about modern re-architecture to eliminate manual interpretation. Answer gives four concrete architecture changes and explicitly defines what human judgment would remain. Appropriately comprehensive for a synthesis/design question.

Prod ops query about triaging intermittent VADIR timeouts causing VCE record lock issues. Answer gives a clear numbered triage playbook with specific callStatus codes, SLA reference, DGI lock behavior, and scope determination. Directly responsive.

Prod ops query about all Ch 33 claims from one day having the same benefit level. Answer gives ordered probable causes with VADIR field references, suggests remediation steps referencing M22-3 QA. Complete.

Prod ops query about DGI having bio info but no military service history. Answer explains the findPeopleByCriteria vs. getServicePeriods distinction and gives three specific causes, plus the VCE fallback. Complete.

Prod ops query about transformation errors from the service record system. Answer identifies Error 16820004 specifically, explains what it means vs. other error codes, gives two likely causes, and provides a concrete playbook. Direct.

Prod ops query about payment history gaps after eMPWR migration. Answer explains what the boundary means, clarifies that BDN historical access should still work, and describes how to identify whether this is expected or a migration defect. Complete.
Prod ops query about doubled VADIR response sizes. Answer explains the two causes, links to SLA risk, lists affected components, and gives monitoring/deduplication guidance. Directly responsive.

Prod ops query about authorization errors in eMPWR for claims from one school. Answer gives the standard troubleshooting steps and escalation path per M22-4, then provides likely school-side causes. Mostly responsive but the escalation path section is generic (matching the M22-4 procedure) rather than tightly scoped to the 'same school' pattern, which should have more prominently elevated the school-specific causes. Minor gap in emphasis.
Prod ops query about failing over the service record system to DR. Answer identifies the three environments, references Section 7.4 specifically, and provides pre/post verification steps. Complete.

Prod ops query about differentiating training, policy change, or data issues from elevated QA error rates. Answer gives a clear pattern-matching framework for all three root causes with specific detection signals. Direct.

Prod ops query about eMPWR maintenance window impact and VCE prep. Answer addresses what's affected (payment auth), what still works (DGI), BT ordering risk, record lock release, and pre/post maintenance steps. Complete.

Contradiction query about 36 vs. 48 months. Answer explains both sides correctly: Ch 33 is 36 months max, 48 months is the combined multi-program cap, and gives the practical reconciliation. Fully responsive.
Contradiction query about BDN vs. eMPWR for master records. Answer explains both are correct for different time periods and use cases, and clarifies how to interpret older M22-4 sections. Complete.
Contradiction query about 90-day minimum and basic training deduction. Answer resolves the apparent contradiction via the 730-day threshold rule and notes the SC disability exception. Complete.

Temporal query about a 2019 claim being picked up now. Answer addresses the specific rule changes since 2019 (benefit level, enrollment verification, eMPWR, delimiting date) and recommends re-pulling service records. Complete.

Temporal query about 2011 separation applying in 2026. Answer explains the 15-year delimiting date for pre-2013 separations, calculates the approximate expiry date, and mentions extension options. Complete.

Temporal query about when enrollment certification rules changed. Answer explains the two-phase rollout dates (Aug 1, 2021 for NCD; Dec 17, 2021 for IHL), what changed, and why older M22-4 sections describe different procedures. Complete.

Negative query asking for procedure for correspondence-only Ch 33 claims. Answer correctly explains there isn't a standard procedure, covers Ch 33 correspondence restrictions, MHA exclusion, and FOCAS reference. However, the answer asserts FOCAS is used without clearly establishing this is definitively specified in M22-4 vs. inferred. Minor uncertainty introduced. Still mostly responsive.

Negative query about appealing a benefit level percentage. Answer correctly says there's no 'benefit level appeal' per se, explains the two actual paths (service record review, NOD), and clarifies what cannot be negotiated. Complete.
Negative query about coding bootcamps under Ch 33. Answer correctly explains the SAA approval requirement, explains VET TEC as an alternative, and gives the VCE the decision tree. Complete.

Ambiguous term query about what 'LTS' means and its role. Answer expands the acronym, explains its role in the claims workflow (VADIR calls, eligibility calculation, VA-ONCE inbox), and notes disambiguation. Complete.

Ambiguous term query about 'master record.' Answer disambiguates across three systems (BDN historically, eMPWR for Ch 33 post-July 2024, DMDC/VADIR for service history) with temporal and chapter-specific context. Complete.

Ambiguous term query about 'character of service' in policy vs. service record data. Answer explains the same concept has different representations, lists the VADIR codes, ties to M22-4 eligibility rules, and flags the blank-defaults-to-honorable risk. Complete.

Synthesis query asking for end-to-end flow from application to first housing allowance payment. Answer walks through all major stages (application, eligibility, COE, school certification, MHA calculation, payment authorization, monthly verification, payment) with specific policy citations. Appropriately comprehensive for a synthesis query.
Synthesis query about all systems a new RPO claims examiner needs. Answer covers eight systems with concise descriptions of each, noting the Ch 33 vs. other chapter distinctions. Complete.
Synthesis query about all ways Ch 33 payment can change without veteran action. Answer identifies seven distinct change mechanisms with policy citations. Directly and comprehensively responsive.
